questions.q: In the Descent of the Ganga, which sage-king performed severe austerities to bring the river Ganga down to earth?
questions.explanation: In the Ramayana, King Bhagiratha undertook long penance to bring the celestial Ganga down to earth.

questions.q: In the story of the Descent of the Ganga, which deity caught the falling river in his matted locks?
questions.explanation: Shiva caught the descending Ganga in his matted hair to break her mighty fall.

questions.q: The Descent of the Ganga is chiefly narrated in which of these texts?
questions.explanation: The episode of Ganga's descent is famously related in the Ramayana, as well as in the Puranas.

questions.q: Bhagiratha sought to bring the Ganga to earth in order to liberate the ashes of whose sixty thousand sons?
questions.explanation: The descent of Ganga liberated the ashes of the sixty thousand sons of King Sagara.

questions.q: Because he caught the river Ganga in his hair, Shiva earns which of these names?
questions.explanation: Shiva is called Gangadhara, meaning 'bearer of the Ganga,' for catching the river in his locks.

questions.q: In the Descent of the Ganga, what type of being is the Ganga described as before reaching the earth?
questions.explanation: The Ganga is described as a celestial (heavenly) river before she descends to the earth.

questions.q: Shiva released the Ganga gently from his hair so that what would not be destroyed by her fall?
questions.explanation: Shiva broke Ganga's powerful descent so that her fall would not shatter the earth.

questions.q: In the Descent of the Ganga, the sixty thousand sons of King Sagara had been reduced to ashes by the wrath of which sage?
questions.explanation: In the Ramayana, Sagara's sons were burnt to ashes by the gaze of the sage Kapila.

questions.q: The river brought down to earth in this episode bears which name?
questions.explanation: The episode concerns the descent of the river Ganga to the earthly realm.

questions.q: In the tradition surrounding the Descent of the Ganga, the river is revered chiefly as what?
options: A river of the dead alone
questions.explanation: The Ganga is revered in the tradition as a sacred river whose waters purify and grant liberation.

questions.q: In the Descent of the Ganga, Bhagiratha was a descendant in the royal line of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha belonged to the Solar dynasty of Ikshvaku, the line of King Sagara and later of Rama.

questions.q: In the Descent of the Ganga, the sixty thousand sons of Sagara were dug up while searching for which lost animal?
questions.explanation: Sagara's sons dug the earth searching for the sacrificial horse of his Ashvamedha yajna.

questions.q: In the Ramayana account, who had concealed Sagara's sacrificial horse, leading his sons to dig toward the netherworld?
questions.explanation: Indra stole and hid the sacrificial horse, which drove Sagara's sons to dig down and disturb the sage Kapila.

questions.q: Before Bhagiratha succeeded, which of his ancestors also strove for the Ganga but did not bring her down?
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha's forefathers Amshuman and Dilipa strove for the Ganga before Bhagiratha finally achieved the descent.

questions.q: In the Descent of the Ganga, whose pleased response to Bhagiratha's penance first granted that Ganga would flow to earth?
questions.explanation: Brahma, pleased by Bhagiratha's austerities, granted the boon that Ganga would descend to the earth.

questions.q: Why was Shiva's intervention needed during the Descent of the Ganga?
options: The river had dried up in the heavens
The earth could not bear the force of her fall
The ocean refused to receive her
questions.explanation: The earth could not withstand the tremendous force of Ganga's descent, so Shiva broke her fall in his locks.

questions.q: After flowing across the earth, the descended Ganga finally reached which destination to liberate Sagara's sons?
options: The netherworld where their ashes lay
questions.explanation: The Ganga's waters reached the netherworld and touched the ashes of Sagara's sons, granting them liberation.

questions.q: In honour of Bhagiratha, who led her course, the river Ganga is also known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Ganga is also called Bhagirathi after King Bhagiratha, who guided her to the ashes of his ancestors.

questions.q: How did Bhagiratha guide the flowing Ganga across the land in the traditional Ramayana accounts?
options: He rode ahead in his chariot, which she followed
He struck the ground with a trident
He blew a conch shell to summon her
He shot arrows to mark her path
questions.explanation: In the Ramayana, Bhagiratha rode ahead in his chariot and the Ganga followed his path to the sea and netherworld.

questions.q: As the Ganga flowed across the earth, she is said to have flooded the sacrificial ground of which sage, who then drank her up?
questions.explanation: The sage Jahnu swallowed the Ganga when she flooded his sacrifice, later releasing her at Bhagiratha's plea.

questions.q: Because the sage Jahnu released her, the Ganga gains which additional epithet in the tradition?
questions.explanation: The Ganga is called Jahnavi, 'daughter of Jahnu,' because the sage released her after swallowing her waters.

questions.q: In iconography of Shiva as Gangadhara, the Ganga is typically depicted in which form upon him?
options: A small figure streaming from his matted hair
questions.explanation: Gangadhara Shiva is shown with a small personified Ganga flowing out from his matted locks.

questions.q: In the Puranas, the celestial Ganga is said to have flowed from the toe of which deity before her descent?
questions.explanation: Several Puranas relate that the heavenly Ganga issued from the foot of Vishnu, which gives her the name Vishnupadi.

questions.q: Because of her threefold course through heaven, earth, and the netherworld, the Ganga earns which epithet?
questions.explanation: The Ganga is called Tripathaga, 'she who travels the three paths,' flowing through the three worlds.

questions.q: Who was the father of the sixty thousand sons whose deliverance prompted the Descent of the Ganga?
questions.explanation: King Sagara was the father of the sixty thousand sons reduced to ashes, whose liberation Bhagiratha sought.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, why did Bhagiratha first propitiate Shiva before the Ganga's descent, according to Brahma's counsel?
options: Only Shiva could heat her cold waters
Only Shiva could light the netherworld
Only Shiva could lift Kapila's curse
Only Shiva could endure the force of her fall
questions.explanation: Brahma told Bhagiratha that none but Shiva could bear the tremendous force of Ganga's fall to earth.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata and Puranas, the goddess Ganga is also remembered as the mother of which warrior, born to King Shantanu?
questions.explanation: Ganga, as wife of King Shantanu, is the mother of Bhishma in the Mahabharata tradition.

questions.q: In the geography of the Descent of the Ganga, the river is said to have first fallen upon which heights among Shiva's locks?
questions.explanation: The Ganga first fell into Shiva's matted locks upon the Himalayan heights before being released gently to the plains.

questions.q: In the Descent of the Ganga, the great length of Bhagiratha's penance reflects which quality for which his name became proverbial?
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha's name became proverbial for 'Bhagiratha prayatna,' a heroic and unrelenting effort, after his long penance.

questions.q: In tradition, the point where the descending Ganga is said to enter the plains from the mountains is identified with which holy region?
questions.explanation: The Ganga's emergence from the Himalayas is traditionally linked to the Gangotri and Haridwar region where she reaches the plains.
